metrics.narrative: TTI has quantified the scale of the problem, not yet a formal ROI: at the 12 Jan 2026 RFP briefing Duncan Tennyson's team put ANZ indirect spend at roughly $110M via POs and $130M via non-PO invoices, covering 10 power users and ~200 contributors. In the 2 Jul 2025 demo, TTI's team described a concrete pain point ('there is a need to manage one budget across multiple vendors within the requisition and purchase order system' and difficulty 'tracking budget utilization and monitoring potential overutilization'). No buyer has yet stated a quantified savings or ROI figure for Zycus specifically; converting the spend scale into a dollarized business case is the open move.

champion.narrative: Tej Ram (developing) has been the constant, cooperative contact across an 18-month cycle: attended the original Jan 2025 demo, was re-introduced post a prior rep's exit (Sep 2025), personally supplied the Nissan and TataPlay references, introduced Zycus's SEQ partner contact directly with a phone number on 18 Jun 2026, and confirmed to Carl Kimball on 30 Jun 2026 that references were complete and the process in final stages. What is still missing: no evidence Tej or Duncan Tennyson (also technical-evaluator, RFP project manager) has advocated for Zycus at the CFO level, or that either has access to the Melbourne CFO who makes the final call. Champion strength is real but confined to the technical-evaluator layer.

competition.narrative: TTI ran a broad RFP field naming Coupa, GEP, iValua and SAP Ariba as early as 13 Jan 2026 ('Zycus seen as strong solution... vs Coupa/iValua/Ariba'). By 11 May 2026 TTI down-selected to a final 2, with Next_Step__c flagging 'GEP, iValua strong threats here.' SAP Ariba has not resurfaced since Jan 2026 and appears dormant. Most recently (1 Jul 2026), the likely scope pivot to S2C-only was framed as putting 'Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa,' since Coupa's relative strength sits in the P2P layer being deferred to 2027. The wedge: lean into iContract/iSource/Merlin AI differentiation for the S2C-only scope while GEP/iValua remain the live finalist-stage threats.

identify_pain.narrative: TTI's core pain is modernization risk: it runs on an aging on-premise SAP ECC6 with a planned migration to S/4HANA (design starting Q3 2026, per the 12 Dec 2025 and 12 Jan 2026 calls), and per Next_Step__c (1 Jul 2026) 'this is all coming from the risk they feel moving from ECC to S4Hana' -- the stated reason TTI is now pivoting scope from full S2P to S2C-only. Secondary pain, from the 2 Jul 2025 demo, is the inability to manage a single budget across multiple vendors and purchase requests without manual tracking. TTI also flagged nascent ESG policy needs (12 Dec 2025 call: 'ESG is on the road map... recently recruited a sourcing manager'). Duncan Tennyson and Tej Ram are the named owners of this pain internally.

paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, legal, or procurement/InfoSec activity has surfaced yet; the deal is still pre-contract. The immediate paper-adjacent ask is the RACI and phased implementation/project plan that Duncan and Tej requested on 1 Jul 2026, explicitly because 'all the other vendors gave them' one and Zycus has not. Contracting mechanics will only become visible once the CFO's scope decision (week of 13 Jul 2026) lands and negotiation begins; delivering the RACI now is the visibility-creating move.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is a Melbourne-based CFO who initiated the RFP ('CFO requested to initiate RFP' - 12 Dec 2025 call) and who alone will decide scope and vendor the week of 13 Jul 2026 ('meeting CFO week of 13th July to decide on scope' - Next_Step__c, 1 Jul 2026). Zycus has never engaged this person directly: 'Unable to meet CPO as it was conflict of interest during RFP process' (10 Feb 2026). Global sign-off on costings is also required before contract per the 12 Dec 2025 call. This is the single biggest live gap: the decision-maker's meeting happens in days with no Zycus voice in the room.

decision_process.narrative: The call sequence itself maps the process: broad RFP field (Jan 2026) -> written proposals (Feb 2026) -> onsite demos (5-12 Mar 2026) -> clarification rounds (18 Mar 2026) -> BAFO/vendor-decision timeline revisions (27 Mar, 10 Apr, 28 Apr 2026) -> down-select to 2 finalists with sandbox/commercials/implementation planning (11 May 2026) -> reference checks (Nissan, TataPlay; cleared by 30 Jun 2026) -> now a scope decision with the CFO (week of 13 Jul 2026). The timeline has slipped repeatedly (award date moved from March to May to June to now), showing a real but recurringly optimistic buyer clock. Global sign-off on costings still sits above the CFO's scope call per the 12 Dec 2025 discussion.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented from the formal RFP run via the relevant stakeholder: 6 functional questionnaires plus a separate pricing/contract track, assessed on submission completeness, technical/functional fit, references, the implementation program (including a phased approach and resourcing plan), and commercial terms (license, implementation, change management, post-go-live support). Confirmed in detail on the 12 Jan 2026 RFP briefing call by Duncan Tennyson: 'we'll get our SMEs looking at the technical and functional responses... we're very interested in the implementation program.' As of 1 Jul 2026, TTI is explicitly benchmarking Zycus's RACI/project plan against what GEP, iValua and Coupa already submitted.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: TTI has down-selected Zycus into the final two vendors and cleared reference checks, but the unengaged CFO decides scope and vendor the week of 13 Jul 2026 without a Zycus pricing/ROI conversation, and rivals GEP/iValua/Coupa have already delivered the RACI and project plan Zycus still owes.

deal_risk.text: The 30 Jul 2026 close date precedes any contracting activity and falls only 17 days after the CFO's scope decision, an unrealistic runway for negotiation and paperwork.
The scope pivot to S2C-only is explicitly driven by TTI's anxiety over its SAP ECC6-to-S4HANA migration; Brett Campbell, the EAI Manager who owns that integration risk, remains unengaged by Zycus.

win_position.text: TTI down-selected Zycus to its final two vendors on 11 May 2026 and told Carl Kimball on 30 Jun 2026 that reference checks (Nissan, TataPlay) are complete and the process is in its final stages.

The Melbourne CFO, who alone decides scope and vendor the week of 13 Jul 2026, has never met or been briefed by Zycus, leaving the case entirely in Tej Ram's and Duncan Tennyson's hands as internal advocates.
GEP, iValua and Coupa have already given TTI a RACI and phased implementation plan; Zycus has not, which risks looking under-prepared exactly as the CFO compares submissions.

deal_momentum.text: The deal is progressing through real milestones (down-select, references, sandbox/implementation planning) rather than stalling, with a live buyer call as recently as 1 Jul 2026.
A likely scope pivot from full S2P to S2C-only (announced 1 Jul 2026) would shrink the deal from $220,000 toward roughly $110,000-$130,000 and push procure-to-pay revenue into 2027.

customer_commitment.text: Tej Ram has stayed engaged and cooperative across an 18-month cycle, personally supplying references and introducing Zycus's SEQ partner contact on 18 Jun 2026.
Commitment is confined to the technical-evaluator layer; there is no evidence of Duncan Tennyson or Tej Ram advocating for Zycus at the CFO level.

hard.ais_why:
• Per the 11 May 2026 rep note: 'confirmed by email- shortlisted to 2 for further implementation plans, commercials and hands on sandbox- need urgent and solid local reference. GEP, iValua strong threats here.' Across 3,282 chars of NextStep spanning Jan 2025 to May 2026 ‚Äî covering RFP, demos, onsite presentations and shortlisting ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic, or autonomous despite Merlin, Merlin Intake, and Agentic AI being in product scope.
• On 5 Mar 2026: 'Onsite presentation/demo for 3 hours scheduled for 10th March.' On 12 Mar 2026: 'Completed onsite demo, they have a few more vendors to review.' On 12 Dec 2025: 'CFO requested to initiate RFP around 5th January, looking to decide on a vendor by April and start implementation.' The decision framework is CFO-led, vendor-comparison-driven (vs GEP/iValua/Coupa/Ariba) with no AI-specific evaluation criterion captured.
• Confidence is Medium: heavy AI product loadout (3 AI SKUs) signals Zycus positioning, but the buyer-side dialogue captured across 16 months is pure traditional S2P RFP ‚Äî onsite demos, BAFO, 'hands on sandbox', local reference. The 4/10 score reflects AI in scope without AI in conversation; would move up only if hands-on sandbox produces AI-specific buyer engagement.
